name: stage-transpose-tap-target
note: P1-2 (SDD persona goalpost): the Stage's transpose steppers (#pDown/#pUp, the Key row inside the gear sheet #pSpeed) are a safety-critical control - a mis-tap changes the performed key mid-song. Prior UAT (F7, see persona-performer-setlist.json) already moved them OUT of the crowded top bar next to Close; this goalpost is the remaining gap - the steppers themselves render below the 44px tap-target floor once the gear sheet is opened. Red-first: pre-fix each stepper's own box is < 44x44 (the shared .psBtn settings-row treatment: ~8px padding + .78rem text).

steps.label: seed a saved custom song with a chord sheet so Stage has content
steps.js: (function(){try{localStorage.setItem('roadcase-ukulele-gcea.custom.v1', JSON.stringify([{id:'transposeTapSong', t:'Transpose Tap Song', a:'Me', y:2026, d:'Mine', custom:true, seq:['C','G','Am','F'], sheet:[['Verse','[C] a [G] b [Am] c [F] d']]}]));return true}catch(e){return false}})()

steps.label: gear sheet is open and the Key row's steppers are visible
transpose steppers live in the gear sheet, not the top bar next to Close (away from a mis-tap while reaching for Close)
each transpose stepper meets the 44x44 tap-target floor (FAILS pre-fix: shared .psBtn ~8px padding + .78rem text is well under 44px tall)
the two steppers keep >=8px of daylight between them so a Key-down/Key-up mis-tap can't slide from one into the other
